key_points: The cell is the smallest structural and functional unit capable of performing all life processes
Cell theory was developed by Schleiden and Schwann and later modified by Virchow
Cells may differ in size, shape and function depending on their roles
The plasma membrane follows the fluid mosaic model composed of phospholipids and proteins
Transport across membranes occurs by diffusion, osmosis and active transport
Plasmolysis occurs when plant cells lose water in a hypertonic solution
The nucleus contains nucleoplasm, nucleolus and chromosomes carrying DNA
DNA controls protein synthesis and hereditary characteristics
Endoplasmic reticulum forms an intracellular transport network
Rough ER synthesizes proteins while smooth ER synthesizes lipids
Golgi apparatus modifies, packages and distributes proteins and lipids
Mitochondria contain their own DNA and ribosomes and are semi-autonomous
Lysosomes carry hydrolytic enzymes responsible for intracellular digestion
Plastids such as chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis
Vacuoles regulate osmotic balance and maintain turgor pressure in plant cells
Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles
Eukaryotic cells possess a well-defined nucleus and complex organelles
Cell division ensures growth, repair, reproduction and genetic continuity
Mitosis produces genetically identical daughter cells
Meiosis produces genetically diverse gametes with reduced chromosome number
examples: Example of eukaryotic cell: Human cell
Organelle responsible for protein synthesis: Ribosome
Organelle responsible for packaging materials: Golgi apparatus
formulas: Diffusion → Passive movement from high concentration to low concentration
Osmosis → Diffusion of water through a selectively permeable membrane
Active Transport → Movement of substances against concentration gradient using ATP
Mitosis → Produces two identical diploid daughter cells
Meiosis → Produces four haploid daughter cells
exam_tips: Understand the fluid mosaic model of plasma membrane
Learn differences between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells
Practice labelled diagrams of plant cell, animal cell and mitochondria
Remember functions of all cell organelles clearly
Understand processes like plasmolysis, diffusion and osmosis with examples
Focus on differences between mitosis and meiosis for exam questions
